(Clone of YARN-796) Allow for (admin) labels on nodes and resource-requests

fields.description: Support node labels store in zookeeper. The main scenario for this is to give a way to decouple yarn with HDFS. Since nodelabel is a very important data for yarn, if hdfs down, yarn will fail to start up,too. So it is meaningful for make yarn much independence when user serve both yarn and HDFS.

fields.summary: Node Label should support pluggable storage

Hi [~wjlei], Thanks for working on this jira, I had a breif discussion on the this jira offline with [~wangda], my main interests with this was for the issue reported in  YARN-4881. but seems like there is configuration like *"yarn.node-labels.fs-store.retry-policy-spec"* should mitigate the issue to pretty decent extent. And an possible limitation for Zookeeper store would be based on YARN-3409 we would introduce multiple labels per node, so we were little skeptical about the performance in the case of a large cluster. So i thought of waiting till some progress in YARN-3409 (Constraint labels) jira which i am planning to start and then take the decision for this.
At the same time would like to know your scenario for making use of Zookeeper ? Can you define more about your use case?

Hi, [~Naganarasimha], Thanks for reply. The main scenario for this is to give a way to decouple yarn with HDFS. Since nodelabel is a very important data for yarn, if hdfs down, yarn still fail to start up. So I think this change is meaningful for make yarn much independence when user serve both yarn and HDFS. I make a way to beyond the zknode 1MB storage limitation. In UT, it can support 100000 nodemanager, recover all 100000 node nodelabel cost 4 seconds. It is really efficiency and reliable.

Thanks for multiple patches [~wjlei],
bq. The main scenario for this is to give a way to decouple yarn with HDFS. Since nodelabel is a very important data for yarn, if hdfs down, yarn still fail to start up. So I think this change is meaningful for make yarn much independence when user serve both yarn and HDFS. 
Thanks for the update, Are you targeting for the use case of cloud ? In most cases HDFS and YARN are tied up and also NodeLabelStore supports Local file system too though its not reliable.
IMO think its *not* a must have but good to have feature. So let me take a look at it.
bq. I make a way to beyond the zknode 1MB storage limitation. In UT, it can support 100000 nodemanager, recover all 100000 node nodelabel cost 4 seconds. It is really efficiency and reliable.
I was targetting more towards Constraint labels where in approx 50 cluster constraint node labels and decent cluster size of 5000 and each node having around 10 constraint labels. so did some maths on it and should not impact so much.
Give me some time, Let me go over the patch and give my views/comments
Sure, [~Naganarasimha], just ask me if you has some confusion. The change is for the use case of cloud and zookeeper is a good tool to support fail-over and  high reliable.
HI, [~Naganarasimha], Do you have any update  for this? or need I to add some document?

Sorry for the late response, I just discussed with [~subru], and talked to [~naganarasimha_gr@apache.org] few months before.

Thanks for taking up this JIRA, [~wjlei], I would suggest:
- If you want it works with WASB file system (or other file system doesn't support append), you can try to use NonAppendableFSNodeLabelStore.  
- If you don't want YARN fails because of HDFS failures, you can setup yarn.node-labels.fs-store.retry-policy-spec 
- Reasonable number of nodes and reasonable frequency of node label updating is already supported by existing stores. There will be a lot of issue if #nodemanager goes beyond 20000+, for example, scheduler itself becomes a bottleneck.

I would say adding the new ZK store impl will add extra overhead to code maintenance. And since pluggable node label store is supported, if you really want to run a ZK store, you can configure it in yarn-site.xml. So I suggest to keep this open until this becomes a common requirement for node label.

Sorry [~wjlei], my bad! thought had replied for it. As [~wangda] mentioned had discussecd on this issue and to add on top of this Constraint label[YARN-3409] would add more data for each node, so thought we will wait once the approach is finalized for it and then take account of size of data and frequency of updates and then confirm whether ZK store for Node Labels would be ideal. Because of these open points held on to further review.
Thanks, Wangda, I get your point.

I am closing the issuing for now based on the discussions ([~wjlei], [~leftnoteasy], [~Naganarasimha]) in the JIRA. We can revisit later as [~Naganarasimha] suggested once we have more concrete data points.

Please feel free to reopen if you disagree.

Hi [~wjlei], Thanks for working on this jira, I had a breif discussion on the this jira offline with [~wangda], my main interests with this was for the issue reported in  YARN-4881. but seems like there is configuration like *"yarn.node-labels.fs-store.retry-policy-spec"* should mitigate the issue to pretty decent extent. And an possible limitation for Zookeeper store would be based on YARN-3409 we would introduce multiple labels per node, so we were little skeptical about the performance in the case of a large cluster. So i thought of waiting till some progress in YARN-3409 (Constraint labels) jira which i am planning to start and then take the decision for this.
At the same time would like to know your scenario for making use of Zookeeper ? Can you define more about your use case?

Hi, [~Naganarasimha], Thanks for reply. The main scenario for this is to give a way to decouple yarn with HDFS. Since nodelabel is a very important data for yarn, if hdfs down, yarn still fail to start up. So I think this change is meaningful for make yarn much independence when user serve both yarn and HDFS. I make a way to beyond the zknode 1MB storage limitation. In UT, it can support 100000 nodemanager, recover all 100000 node nodelabel cost 4 seconds. It is really efficiency and reliable.

Thanks for taking up this JIRA, [~wjlei], I would suggest:
- If you want it works with WASB file system (or other file system doesn't support append), you can try to use NonAppendableFSNodeLabelStore.  
- If you don't want YARN fails because of HDFS failures, you can setup yarn.node-labels.fs-store.retry-policy-spec 
- Reasonable number of nodes and reasonable frequency of node label updating is already supported by existing stores. There will be a lot of issue if #nodemanager goes beyond 20000+, for example, scheduler itself becomes a bottleneck.

I would say adding the new ZK store impl will add extra overhead to code maintenance. And since pluggable node label store is supported, if you really want to run a ZK store, you can configure it in yarn-site.xml. So I suggest to keep this open until this becomes a common requirement for node label.
